title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Go Client Systems · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A platform SDK team integrating a staging control plane must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for deterministic tests, fault injection, contract evidence, and observability, producing fault-injected client verification harness. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go 1.26 net/http fault-injected client verification harness while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fake Transport Fixtures
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client test G-2 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fake Transport Fixtures.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fake Transport Fixtures, keep Fake Transport Fixtures connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

At Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Http Telemetry, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before running it: Go staging control-plane client test G-5 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Record stable client duration, method, route or safe target, status, error type, attempts, bytes, and trace context without high-cardinality secrets. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Http Telemetry, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Logging the complete URL and headers produces ideal HTTP observability. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.

stimulus.title: Http Telemetry failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Logging the complete URL and headers produces ideal HTTP observability. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record stable client duration, method, route or safe target, status, error type, attempts, bytes, and trace context without high-cardinality secrets. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Http Telemetry condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Http Telemetry happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Http Telemetry
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Http Telemetry: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Go staging control-plane client test G-6 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Record stable client duration, method, route or safe target, status, error type, attempts, bytes, and trace context without high-cardinality secrets. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Evidence target: Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Http Telemetry.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Http Telemetry, keep Http Telemetry connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record stable client duration, method, route or safe target, status, error type, attempts, bytes, and trace context without high-cardinality secrets. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
